git_workflow_best_practices.overview: Critical Git workflow practices for CVE remediation to ensure changes are made on the correct branch.

golden_rule.rule: NEVER make changes on the main/master branch. ALWAYS create and checkout the fix branch FIRST.

step.action: Create and checkout the fix branch locally
step.note: Creates and checks out the branch in one command

step.action: Verify you are on the fix branch BEFORE making any changes
step.critical_check: If output is "main" or "master", STOP and create or checkout the fix branch first

step.action: Make changes to package manifest files
step.note: All file modifications happen on the local fix branch

step.action: Commit changes on the fix branch locally

commands.command: git commit -m "fix: upgrade flask to 2.3.3 for CVE-2023-12345"

step.action: Push the fix branch to GitHub only when ready
step.note: This is the first time the remote branch is created. The -u flag sets the upstream tracking branch.

step.action: Create pull request from fix branch to main

anti_pattern.name: modify_main_first
anti_pattern.description: Making changes on main branch then trying to move them to fix branch
anti_pattern.why_wrong: - Pollutes main branch with uncommitted changes - Requires complex git operations to move changes - Risk of accidentally committing to main - Confusing for code review

symptoms.symptom: git status shows modified files on main branch
Need to use git stash to move changes
Accidentally pushed to main instead of fix branch

anti_pattern.name: create_branch_after_changes
anti_pattern.description: Creating branch after making file modifications
anti_pattern.why_wrong: - Changes are already tracked on current branch (main) - Requires git stash or manual copying of changes - Increases the risk of accidental commits on main

anti_pattern.name: push_before_ready
anti_pattern.description: Pushing the branch to GitHub before local work is ready for review
anti_pattern.why_wrong: - Creates unnecessary remote noise - May expose incomplete remediation work - Makes PR timing less intentional
anti_pattern.solution: Keep work local on the fix branch until you are ready to publish it with git push -u origin {branch-name}

item.action_if_wrong: Create or checkout the fix branch immediately

item.check: Ensure the fix branch was created locally before editing files
Ensure working directory is clean before starting

recovery_procedures.title: What to Do If You Made Changes on Main

scenario.name: uncommitted_changes_on_main
scenario.description: You modified files on main but haven't committed yet

recovery_steps.step: Create and checkout fix branch: git checkout -b fix/cve-{CVE-ID}-{package-name}
Apply stashed changes: git stash pop
Verify changes are now on fix branch: git status
Proceed with commit, then push when ready

scenario.name: committed_to_main_by_mistake
scenario.description: You already committed changes to main branch

recovery_steps.step: Create fix branch from current main: git checkout -b fix/cve-{CVE-ID}-{package-name}
Push fix branch when ready: git push -u origin fix/cve-{CVE-ID}-{package-name}
Reset main to remote: git reset --hard origin/main

scenario.warning: Only do this if you haven't pushed to main yet. If you've pushed to main, coordinate with team.

scenario.name: pushed_to_main_by_mistake
scenario.description: You pushed changes to main branch on remote

recovery_steps.step: STOP - Do not proceed without team coordination
Notify team that main was modified
Create fix branch from current main
Revert main to previous state (requires force push)

scenario.warning: This requires force pushing to main. Coordinate with team first.

git_push_guidance.title: Pushing the Local Fix Branch to GitHub

workflow.step: Create and work on the branch locally first
Commit local changes once the remediation is ready
Publish the branch with: git push -u origin fix/cve-{CVE-ID}-{package-name}
Create the pull request from the published branch

git_push_guidance.best_practice: Creating the branch locally first and pushing only at the end is valid and preferred for a cleaner workflow.
git_push_guidance.important_note: The remote branch does not need to exist before you start working. It will be created automatically by the first git push -u origin {branch-name}.

tool.name: create_pull_request
tool.purpose: Creates PR from head branch to base branch

parameter.name: head
parameter.description: The published branch with your changes

parameter.name: base
parameter.description: The branch you want to merge into

best_practices_summary.practice: Create and checkout branch BEFORE any file modifications
Always verify current branch before making changes
Use git branch --show-current frequently during workflow
Keep the fix branch local until the work is ready to publish
Keep main branch clean - never work directly on it
If you make a mistake, use git stash to recover cleanly
